title: Corybas globulus
intro: Corybas globulus, also known as the blue coral orchid, is a species of orchid native to Australia. The health benefits of Corybas globulus include its potential to treat coughs and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties which may aid in wound healing and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Corybas globulus include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Corybas globulus is classified as a species of orchid within the genus Corybas, which is part of the subtribe Corybinae. The genus Corybas is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as 'dwarf or fairy orchids'. These orchids are native to Australia and New Zealand. They belong to the family Diaphantheae and the family Orchidaceae. The classification of Corybas globulus reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the broader orchid family.
